Fine, let him suck up an inordinate amount of your time. It's not going to matter, you'll still make you 350. But if you're trying to make a mill, you can't afford him. Because at the end of the day, something else that was benchmarked that should have happened, ain't going to happen because that guy decided to come in entirely at his discretion on his impulse and talk to you about who the Cleveland Indians traded, and talk to you about that for 20 minutes when it should have taken 10. And at this number, at this number, you can't afford that. That conversation is supposed to be taking place in a particular time, in a particular place, like the Sunday cookout, when you're doing a cookout and you invite your friends over and you talk about who the Cleveland Indians traded and how dumb the trades were. And you've allocated three hours for the cookout, and it really doesn't make any difference what the hell you talk about during the cookout. It's got to go there. It can't be in your office at 10 o'clock in the morning when you were supposed to be finishing something. It can't. Not at seven figures. It can't. But if you don't train him, see, it's not his fault. Because everybody else doesn't care in his world. In fact, most people are happy to have the distraction. See, they not only have taught him that that's normal and customary and okay behavior, they welcome it. They are like, by 10 o'clock in the morning, they're praying some moron shows up with some sort of distraction so that they don't have to do whatever the hell it was they're planning to do and were supposed to be doing. So that's the world he came from. If you want extreme productivity, you have to be willing to disappoint, frustrate, annoy, and inconvenience others. And the more money you want to make, the more of this you've got to be willing to do. And if you're not willing to have him stomping around and grumpy because you wouldn't stop and go have a cup of coffee and talk about who the Cleveland Indians traded, you're doomed. And if you're not willing to have people disappointed with you, you're doomed because they're going to be disappointed. As much as they may even try to understand, they're going to be disappointed. Compartmentalization, what you allow yourself to think about when. See, the guy coming in to talk about the Indians is bad enough, but when you stop what you're doing and just think about the Indians, it's just as bad, accomplish the same thing. So you've got to be able to put it in a box and go to it at its appropriate time. You train your head to do this. It's just a process like anything else. And again, when you live in certain worlds with certain kinds of activities, you do learn to do it. It's just most people don't extend it to everything that they do. So again, to use what you'll see tonight, see, if you go out there, it's more dangerous than it looks. How many of you were at the Super Conference when I was not in so good a shape? Yeah, okay. You really had another one of those the other night. Missed it by that much. The guy behind me got creamed. It's dangerous out there. It's more dangerous than it looks. If you sit up in the clubhouse and sort of see it, you know, it's eight little carts going around, a circle doesn't look like much. But go down and stand by the fence tonight once. It's a lot more dangerous than it looks. And so that three-fifths of a second thing, not only is it the difference between finish and first and second, more times than anybody would care to say, it's the difference between being in a five-force pile up and getting yourself killed, or maybe getting somebody else killed, or not. And it's why at a certain age, most people need to stop, just like in car racing, they need to stop because our reflexes aren't quite as good, our sights not quite as good, you know, etc. But the one thing you damn well better not be doing is thinking about anything else, even for a fleeting three-fifths of a second. I don't care if it was a death in the family that happened four minutes before you either don't get in a bike, or if you are going to get in a bike, you better be able to turn that fight with the spouse, the summons that was delivered, you better be able to turn it off. Now again, everybody does sports, and if you've played sports, or if you now do anything like that, you know this. And for that, you're able to do it, especially if you're doing anything dangerous, because otherwise you probably wouldn't be here, or you'd be in bad shape. Not that they're all avoidable, by the way, but still, you get it. And almost everybody is able to do this with something, where they just shut everything else off, they put a bad thing in a box, but they don't really train themselves to do it with everything you need to be able to do it.
